SEARCH
Package com.mps.deepviolet.api.tls

Class TlsSocket

java.lang.Object
com.mps.deepviolet.api.tls.TlsSocket
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, AutoCloseable
public class TlsSocket extends Object implements Closeable
Pure raw TLS socket implementation for TLS handshake analysis. This class provides full access to TLS metadata without using JSSE, enabling TLS server fingerprinting, SCT extraction, and complete handshake analysis. Features: - Configurable ClientHello (for behavior probes) - Full ServerHello parsing with ALL extensions - Certificate chain extraction - OCSP stapling support - SCT extraction from all sources - No encryption (analysis-only) Usage: 
 try (TlsSocket socket = new TlsSocket("example.com", 443)) {
     TlsMetadata metadata = socket.performHandshake();
     System.out.println(metadata.getVersionString());
     System.out.println(metadata.getCipherSuite());
 }

    • setClientHelloConfig

      public void setClientHelloConfig(ClientHelloConfig config)

      public void setConnectTimeoutMs(int timeout)

      public void setReadTimeoutMs(int timeout)
      Set read timeout in milliseconds.

    • performHandshake

      public TlsMetadata performHandshake() throws TlsException, IOException
      Perform the TLS handshake and return collected metadata. This only performs the unencrypted portion of the handshake.
      Returns:
      Metadata collected from the handshake
      Throws:
      TlsException - on TLS protocol errors
      IOException - on network errors

    • getMetadata

      public TlsMetadata getMetadata()
      Get the metadata from the last handshake.

      public ServerHello getServerHello()
      Get the ServerHello from the last handshake.

      Get server extensions from the last handshake.

      Get the certificate message from the last handshake.

      Get the stapled OCSP response from the last handshake.

      public boolean isClosed()

    • computeTlsFingerprint

      public static String computeTlsFingerprint(String host, int port)
      Compute TLS server fingerprint for a host. This sends 10 different ClientHello probes and combines the responses to create a fingerprint that characterizes the server's TLS behavior.
      Parameters:
      host - Target hostname
      port - Target port
      Returns:
      62-character TLS fingerprint
      See Also:
      • TlsServerFingerprint

    • computeTlsFingerprint

      public static String computeTlsFingerprint(String host)
      Compute TLS server fingerprint for a host with default HTTPS port.
      Parameters:
      host - Target hostname
      Returns:
      62-character TLS fingerprint

      public static TlsMetadata connect(String host, int port) throws TlsException, IOException
      Quick method to connect and get metadata.
      Throws:
      TlsException
      IOException

    • connect

      public static TlsMetadata connect(String host, int port, ClientHelloConfig config) throws TlsException, IOException
      Quick method to connect with custom configuration.
      Throws:
      TlsException
      IOException

    • getCertificateChain

      public static List<X509Certificate> getCertificateChain(String host, int port) throws TlsException, IOException
      Quick method to get certificate chain.
      Throws:
      TlsException
      IOException

    • testFallbackScsv

      public static Boolean testFallbackScsv(String host, int port)
      Test whether the server supports TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV (RFC 7507).

      Sends a TLS 1.1 ClientHello with the TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV sentinel cipher (0x5600) included. If the server responds with an inappropriate_fallback alert (alert description 86), SCSV is supported.

      Parameters:
      host - Target hostname
      port - Target port
      Returns:
      true if SCSV is supported, false if not, null if test was inconclusive

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException

      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Throws:
